seed_path.role: Before any hyphen override runs, the caller seeds [rbp -0x70] from the game-variant descriptor at client +0x19A58 by copying descriptor +0x8 into the inline local that later becomes constructor field +0x30.

evidence.observation: loads RAX = [rsi +0x19A58], the already-mapped game-variant descriptor pointer.
loads RDX = [RAX +0x8], selecting descriptor field +0x8 as the source pointer.
walks that source pointer until NUL, establishing a begin/end pair for a direct string copy.
calls 0x140032150 with RCX = [rbp -0x70], populating the inline local from the descriptor-backed source string.
the earlier game-variant artifact maps descriptor +0x8 as the display-name string pointer and descriptor +0x10 as the slug or canonical-name pointer.
later passes LEA R8,[rbp -0x70] into wrapper 0x14002EC80, proving the seeded inline local is the same value forwarded toward constructor field +0x30.

careful_inference.statement: On the un-overridden path, constructor field +0x30 carries the game-variant display name, not the slug string previously mapped at descriptor +0x10.

override_gate.role: After the display-name seed copy, a second branch checks the string-like object at client +0x19C80 and conditionally overwrites [rbp -0x70] with the single-character literal '-'.

evidence.observation: loads R8 = [rsi +0x19C80], establishing the object inspected before the override runs.
decodes the short/heap-string layout rooted at client +0x19C80 and skips the override branch when the computed length is zero.
loads RDX = 0x140B5E03C for the override literal.
headless C-string read resolves the pointed data to '-'.
creates a temporary stack-local string object at [rsp +0x70] from the '-' literal via helper 0x14002A260.
calls 0x140031970 with RCX = [rbp -0x70], overwriting the earlier seeded inline local with the temporary literal-backed string.
tears down the temporary stack-local string after the overwrite.

careful_inference.statement: The client +0x19C80 branch does not originate the +0x30 field from scratch; it conditionally clobbers a previously seeded display-name value with a hyphen marker.

reconciled_conclusions: The previous '+0x30 is a hyphen marker when client +0x19C80 is non-empty' model was incomplete: the caller first seeds +0x30 from the game-variant display-name pointer at [client +0x19A58] +0x8.
Client +0x19C80 now reads as an override gate, not the original source of the +0x30 string.
This makes constructor field +0x30 a two-state value on the proven path: game display name by default, or '-' when the 0x19C80-backed condition is active.
impact_on_prior_artifacts: Refines win64c-js5-plus30-hyphen-literal-gate by preserving the hyphen override while correcting the earlier omission of the display-name seed copy.

Extends win64c-login-client-19a58-game-variant-table by proving descriptor +0x8 is consumed directly in the JS5/bootstrap wrapper path, while descriptor +0x10 remains the separate slug consumer already mapped elsewhere.
next_targets: Name the semantic role of client +0x19C80 and determine whether it carries a brand, separator, or mode string whose mere non-emptiness forces the hyphen override.
Follow later consumers of constructor field +0x30 to determine whether the display-name versus '-' split controls labeling, routing, or a sentinel branch in the downstream bootstrap object.
Use the improved operand-scan xref helper to recover more exact-address literal consumers under the quick no-analysis workflow instead of relying only on manual windows.
